title: file:// and other schemeless URLs in og:image bypass SSRF guard silently
description: In fetch_card_image() (card.rs line 191), the SSRF guard checks parsed.host_str(). For file:// URLs (e.g., file:///etc/passwd), host_str() returns None, so the guard body is never executed and the URL is passed directly to reqwest::get(). Reqwest rejects file:// at the transport layer so no file is actually read, but the guard's failure-mode is silent skip rather than explicit rejection. Any URL scheme that parses successfully but has no host (file://, data:, custom schemes) silently bypasses the guard. If reqwest is extended or a future code path changes the HTTP client, this silent bypass becomes a live vulnerability. The guard should explicitly require the https scheme before checking the host.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-3mgw.16 (same file:// SSRF guard bypass).
